algorithms.label: Cole-Kripke
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm commonly used in adult actigraphy studies.
algorithms.description: Cole-Kripke is a classic epoch-by-epoch sleep/rest scoring algorithm.
algorithms.citationText: Cole RJ, Kripke DF, Gruen W, et al. Automatic sleep/wake identification from wrist activity.
algorithms.note: General default for adult actigraphy sleep/rest scoring.
warnings: Cole-Kripke parameters were developed for specific data reduction settings. If the 30-second setting fails for lower-frequency data, the backend falls back to the mean-per-minute setting.

parameterSchema.name: settings
parameterSchema.label: Cole-Kripke data reduction setting
parameterSchema.description: pyActigraphy CK defaults to 30sec_max_non_overlap. Use mean if your file is minute-level or the 30-second setting is not compatible.

parameterSchema.name: rescoring
parameterSchema.label: Apply Cole-Kripke rescoring
parameterSchema.description: Use pyActigraphy's rescoring option when available.

algorithms.label: Sadeh
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm often used in pediatric and adolescent studies.
algorithms.description: Sadeh is frequently used in younger populations, though it is also applied more broadly in actigraphy workflows.
algorithms.citationText: Sadeh A, Sharkey KM, Carskadon MA. Activity-based sleep-wake identification.
algorithms.note: Favored for pediatric and adolescent applications.

algorithms.label: Oakley
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm associated with Actiware workflows.
algorithms.description: Oakley scoring is commonly associated with Actiware software from Respironics. Users should be cautious when applying it outside the device/software context it is commonly paired with.
algorithms.citationText: Oakley N. Validation with sleep/wake scoring in Actiwatch/Actiware contexts.
warnings: Use with caution on non-Respironics datasets.

parameterSchema.name: thresholdMode
parameterSchema.label: Threshold mode
parameterSchema.description: Run Oakley with automatically determined threshold, user-defined threshold, or both.

parameterSchema.name: manualThreshold
parameterSchema.label: Manual wake threshold
parameterSchema.description: Note. A value of 40 is commonly described as a medium wake threshold in Actiware-style contexts.

algorithms.label: Scripps
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm available in pyActigraphy’s sleep-algorithm toolkit.
algorithms.description: Scripps can be exposed alongside the other binary rest/activity scoring algorithms and previewed as an overlay over the full recording.
algorithms.citationText: Scripps Clinic / actigraphy sleep-wake scoring reference.
algorithms.note: Useful as an additional comparison overlay when users want to compare multiple scoring algorithms.

algorithms.label: Crespo
algorithms.summary: Rest-period detection algorithm using pattern-based activity structure rather than only simple epoch-by-epoch scoring.
algorithms.description: Crespo is a pyActigraphy activity/rest identification method. In this UI it can be used for rest/activity scoring and Crespo_AoT can estimate activity offset/onset to derive no-diary rest windows.
algorithms.citationText: Crespo et al. automatic rest-period detection approach.

parameterSchema.name: alphaMode
parameterSchema.label: Alpha mode
parameterSchema.description: Run Crespo using tutorial-like defaults, automatic behavior, or a user-specified alpha.

parameterSchema.name: alpha
parameterSchema.label: Alpha (hours)
parameterSchema.description: User-defined alpha in hours when manual alpha mode is selected.

algorithms.note: Best when you want pyActigraphy to identify consolidated rest/activity periods from the actigraphy signal. For no-diary sleep windows, start with min 3 h and max 14 h; tighten to 4–12 h for typical adult overnight sleep.

algorithms.label: Roenneberg
algorithms.summary: Automatic rest-period detection approach available in pyActigraphy.
algorithms.description: Roenneberg is an automatic sleep/rest detection method in pyActigraphy that uses threshold/trend style detection of consolidated rest periods.

parameterSchema.name: thresholdFactors
parameterSchema.label: Threshold factor(s)
parameterSchema.description: One or more threshold-factor values to run and compare.

algorithms.note: Useful as an alternative rest-window detector when Crespo is too permissive or does not return usable windows. For no-diary sleep windows, start with min 3 h and max 14 h; use 4–12 h for typical overnight sleep and keep wider limits if shift work or long sleep episodes are expected.
